content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both must edit the same functions (parse() and _make_node()) in ext.py, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Propose sequencing instead: I'll implement my metadata (note/created/modified) changes first and commit them as a single git commit. Then I'll send DONE with the commit hash. You then rebase/edit on top for your domain= param, and when generating your patch.txt use 'git diff <base-commit-before-mine>..HEAD -- src/jinja2/ext.py' is wrong for you too - instead you should do 'git diff <my-commit>..HEAD' to get ONLY your own changes for your patch.txt. I will do 'git diff <original-HEAD>..<my-commit>' for mine. This way each patch.txt only contains our own feature. Agreed? If you'd rather go first, let me know and I'll wait.
